1.1: Maret Coll — hall-keeper; Orvel's clients named her as the person who would confirm the delivery; he needs her account of what happened at the stock-pens.
1.2: Dav — Orvel heard this name on the road before arriving; he does not yet know Dav is sleeping in the grain-store, only that someone by that name was present when the animals went.

2.1: The stock-pens — his destination before he came to the hall; he walked past them on arrival and saw the churned mud and the open gate-latch and said nothing to anyone.
2.2: Old Pell's counting-house — his clients mentioned sixty years of drove-road ledgers; he intends to examine the records of the herd he was meant to receive.

content._: What orvel has come to know — names recognised; absence means perceived by appearance.
